title: Pentagonia amazonica
taxonomy: Pentagonia amazonica is a species of plant in the Rubiaceae family. It is classified as a member of the Apocynaceae family under the alternate classification system. However, the Rubiaceae classification seems more accurate in most of the available information. This plant is native to the tropical regions of the Americas. Further classification details may vary depending on the source.

Traditionally, the plant has been used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and digestive problems. intro: Pentagonia amazonica, also known as Amazonian pentagonia, is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest, belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to treat various ailments including wounds, infections, and diseases related to oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pentagonia amazonica include pentagynine, an alkaloid that has been shown to exhibit significant pharmacological activities, as well as other compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids. In traditional medicine, Pentagonia amazonica is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are consumed to treat a range of health issues, and it is also used in folk medicine to make salves and ointments for wound healing and skin conditions.
